title: Gounder Gives Lowdown on Ebola, Peptides, and Colorectal Screenings
summary: KFF Health News' editor-at-large for public health discussed peptides, colorectal cancer screening, and Ebola in TV appearances this week.

title: Budget-Strapped Montana Will Stress-Test Trump’s Medicaid Work Rules
summary: Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

title: After Her Bout of Amnesia, a $59,000 Billing Dispute Wouldn’t Go Away
summary: Last spring, a woman started exhibiting unusual memory problems after a hike in Arizona. It turns out she was experiencing a disorder called transient global amnesia. She has fully recovered, but a dispute over nearly $60,000 in hospital charges has been a source of stress for over a year.

title: A Trump Stronghold Grapples With Health Risks of ICE Detention Sites
summary: Several rural communities were thrust into a charged national debate over the Trump administration’s mass deportation strategy when federal officials sought to place new detention centers in them. In Social Circle, Georgia, locals fear the effort will overburden its modest healthcare infrastructure.

title: In a Vaccine-Skeptical California County, a Potential Playbook To Contain Measles
summary: Conservative Shasta County stopped a measles outbreak from spreading, enlisting teachers, church leaders, and other trusted community members to get the public on board with health guidelines. Infectious disease specialists say the successful effort could be a guide for other communities struggling to contain the highly contagious virus.

title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
summary: The "KFF Health News Minute" brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

title: Nurse Convicted in Patient’s Death Turns Fatal Drug Error Into a Cautionary Tale
summary: RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide for accidentally dispensing a deadly drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.

title: Montana Hurries To Adopt Trump’s Medicaid Work Rules Amid Budget Woes
summary: The state is ramping up to implement the federal work requirements six months ahead of the deadline. But Montana is one of several states already struggling to pay for health services.

title: Readers Address Drugged Driving, Suicide Prevention, Worker Shortages
summary: KFF Health News gives readers a chance to comment on a recent batch of stories.
